content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 28, 2006


Bryant W. Plitt
7-91874
Post Office Box 8503
C-4, 204up
Coalinga, CA 93210

Re: Our File No. G-06-035

Dear Mr. Plitt:

We have received additional correspondence from you requesting that we direct information on California's "Three Strikes Law." As we advised you in previous correspondence, your request is not within the purview of the Fair Political Practices Commission. Therefore, we are unable to further assist you.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Joan Giannetta
Legal Assistant, Legal Division

JG/rd
I:\AdviceLtrs\06-035

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 10, 2006


Bryant W. Plitt
7-91874
Post Office Box 8503
C-4, 204up
Coalinga, CA 93210

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. G-06-035

Dear Mr. Plitt:

This letter is in response to your request regarding Assembly Bill No. 50 (2005-2006 Reg. Sess.) as it relates to California's "Three Strikes Law."

QUESTION

Your questions pertain to the role the Fair Political Practices Commission ("FPPC") has in the passage of Assembly Bill 50 (Leno), which if passed by the Legislature, will amend sections of the California Penal Code regarding sexual offenders.

ANALYSIS

The role of the FPPC in state government is addressed on our web site as follows:

The Mission of the FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION is to promote the integrity of representative state and local government in California through fair, impartial interpretation and enforcement of political campaign, lobbying, and conflict of interest laws.
(Emphasis added.)

File No. G-06-035
Page No. 2

The Fair Political Practices Commission was created by the Political Reform Act
(the "Act") of 1974, a ballot initiative passed by voters. The FPPC investigates alleged
violations of the Political Reform Act, and enforces conflict of interest codes.
Unfortunately, your question regarding AB 50 does not fall within the purview of the
Act.

I encourage you to direct your comments regarding SB 50 to the state legislative
representatives in your district. I would also advise you to monitor a proposed ballot
initiative entitled, "The Three Strikes Reform Act of 2006," which has been filed for title
and summary with the California Office of the Attorney General by Los Angeles District
Attorney Steve Cooley and Brian Dunn, Esquire.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Joan Giannetta
Legal Assistant, Legal Division

JG/rd
I:\AdviceLtrs\06-035

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear in Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
